title: 2025 in film - Wikipedia
text:
2025 Films and Box Office Highlights from Wikipedia:

Highest-Grossing Films:

The top films of 2025 by worldwide box office were:

1. Ne Zha 2 - $2.215 billion (Beijing Enlight)
2. Zootopia 2 - $1.870 billion (Disney)
3. Avatar: Fire and Ash - $1.490 billion (20th Century Studios)
4. Lilo & Stitch - $1.038 billion (Disney)
5. A Minecraft Movie - $961 million (Warner Bros.)

Notable Box Office Records:

Ne Zha 2 achieved remarkable milestones as the highest-grossing animated film of all time and became the first non-English language film to surpass $2 billion globally. It also holds the distinction of being the highest-grossing film in a single market.

Zootopia 2 claimed the fourth-largest worldwide opening in cinema history at $560.3 million and became Disney's highest-grossing animated film, surpassing Inside Out 2.

Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle earned approximately $780 million to become the highest-grossing Japanese film of all time and achieved significant records in both Japan and the United States.

Lilo & Stitch became the highest-grossing live-action animated film ever made, reaching $1 billion globally.

Key Takeaway: The 2025 film year was defined by the dominance of international animated films, particularly from China and Japan, challenging traditional Hollywood dominance in the global box office.
